output.title: Security Deposit Not Returned? The 3-Email Demand Sequence for Frustrated Tenants
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what deductions are illegal, and get a free security deposit dispute letter from DepositBack.
content_outline: Introduction: Why your landlord is withholding your security deposit and why you need to act fast
Know Your Rights: How long your landlord has to return your deposit and when penalties apply
Illegal Deductions: What a landlord can and cannot deduct from your security deposit
The 3-Email Recovery Sequence: Subject lines and body copy to demand your deposit back
Final CTA: How to generate a state-specific security deposit dispute letter in minutes
key_points: Email 1 - Subject: 'My landlord won't give me my deposit back' | Body: Acknowledge the frustration, state your move-out date, and cite the missed legal deadline for your state.
Email 2 - Subject: 'Landlord withholding security deposit for cleaning?' | Body: Challenge unfair deductions by distinguishing normal wear and tear from actual damage, and request an itemized list.

Email 3 - Subject: 'Security deposit not returned—formal dispute letter attached' | Body: Escalate with a formal demand letter generated by DepositBack, warning of small claims court and statutory penalties.
Urgency Hook: Every day you delay, your landlord keeps your money interest-free; state deadlines can trigger double or triple damages in your favor.
SEO Anchor: Target keywords including 'can landlord keep security deposit,' 'what to do when landlord doesn't return deposit,' and 'tenant rights security deposit' naturally within copy.
Trust Signal: Emphasize that DepositBack letters are state-specific, legally structured, and sent in under 5 minutes with no attorney fees required.
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord hold your money hostage. Generate your free, state-specific security deposit demand letter with DepositBack now and recover what's yours.
output.target_audience: Problem-aware US renters who are frustrated because their landlord is wrongfully withholding, delaying, or refusing to return their security deposit
